subject: Science
chapter_name: Exploring Substances: Acidic, Basic and Neutral
overall_learning_objectives: Identify acidic, basic, and neutral substances using indicators
Understand the properties of acids and bases
Differentiate between natural and synthetic indicators
Perform simple tests with litmus paper and turmeric
Understand the concept of neutralization in daily life
Recognize common substances used at home as acids or bases
Maintain safety while handling chemical substances

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Acids and Bases
topics_covered: Taste of common substances (lemon, curd, baking soda)
Definition of Acids and Bases in simple terms
student_activities: List 5 sour and 5 bitter substances found in the kitchen
Discuss the 'soapy' feel of soap water
assessment: Classifying a list of items as sour/bitter/other

lesson_plan.title: Natural Indicators: Litmus
topics_covered: Effect of acidic and basic solutions on blue/red litmus
student_activities: Test lemon juice and soap solution with litmus paper
assessment: Spot test: Identifying unknown liquids using litmus

lesson_plan.title: Other Natural Indicators
student_activities: Observe the change in turmeric when soap is applied
assessment: Short written quiz on color changes

lesson_plan.title: Neutral Substances and pH Basics
topics_covered: Introduction to the concept of the pH scale (Simplified)
teaching_strategy: Logic-based discussion (Why doesn't water change litmus color?)
Visual aid showing a simple pH 1-14 color bar
student_activities: Test distilled water and common salt solution
Draw a color-coded pH scale in notebooks
assessment: Exit slip: Name two neutral substances

lesson_plan.title: The Process of Neutralization
topics_covered: Reaction between an acid and a base
student_activities: Observe the temperature change during a demonstration
Write the word equation: Acid + Base -> Salt + Water
assessment: Individual worksheet on the neutralization reaction

lesson_plan.title: Neutralization in Everyday Life
student_activities: Roleplay: A doctor treating a patient with acidity
Case study: How to treat acidic soil
assessment: Group presentation on one real-life application
Teacher evaluation of the application logic

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Assessment
topics_covered: Summary of Acids, Bases, and Neutralizers

teaching_aids: Litmus paper (Red/Blue), Turmeric, China Rose petals
Common samples: Lemon, Vinegar, Soap solution, Baking soda, Milk of Magnesia
teacher_tip: Focus heavily on the 'Color Change' table for litmus, turmeric, and china rose, as students often swap the results for acids and bases. Use mnemonics like 'BAR' (Blue to Red is Acid) to help them remember.
